alkylation reagent: alkylated with 100 mM iodoacetamide in the dark for 45 min at room temperature
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 100 mM iodoacetamide in the dark for 45 min at room temperature
cleavage agent: adding 1 µg trypsin (Promega #V5111) for overnight incubation at 37 °C
collision energy: fragmentation by collision-induced dissociation (CID) at 35% normalized collision energy in the ion trap
fractionation method: the entire lane excised for subsequent trypsin digestion and LC-MS/MS
instrument: coupled to an Orbitrap Elite Mass Spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc.)
reduction reagent: reduced with 10 mM dithiothreitol at 50 °C for 45 min
reduction concentration: reduced with 10 mM dithiothreitol at 50 °C for 45 min
